name: Bandel, John Martin
motto: "He never said a foolish thing, Nor ever did a wise one." —Rochester.
clubs: Assistant Business Manager "News-Letter," '99-'00, and Business Manager, '00-'01
Secretary and Alternate Senior Debating Team (winning)
description: One of the smooth-tongued gang frequenting the J. H. U. Carries always a small trunk in which he keeps the earnings of his persuasive game—"News-Letter" subscriptions. He is, moreover, suspected of concealing in this grip a gold brick which he offers for sale. Wears a vertically striped suit, but another is being made with the stripes running crosswise. A bold, bad man.

name: Bernheim, Bertram Moses
motto: "Rude am I in my speech, And little blessed with the soft phrase of peace;" —Shakespeare.
description: Average height, pale green eyes, mop-head. Well known in Kentucky as a moonshiner, and has scar on forehead result of encounters with revenue officers. Wanted for complicity in the Goebel case.

name: Baker, Hollis Roswell
motto: "Even a fool when he holdeth his peace is counted wise." —Proverbs.
description: No definite accusation brought against him. Preserves an ominous silence which is in itself suspicious. Agency keeps an unrelenting eye fixed firmly upon him, and expects to hear from him daily. ABERDEEN.

name: Baldauf, Leon Kahn
motto: "O' he's as tedious As a tired horse, a railing wife; Worse than a smoky house:—I had rather live With cheese and garlic in a windmill, far, Than feed on cates, and have him talk to me, In any summer-house in Christendom." —Shakespeare.
description: Has been committed four times on charge "Disturbing the Peace," especially in Public Libraries. Readily detected by swaggering and noisy walk. Has been caught preaching atheism to Freshmen in Trophy Room, and has anarchistical tendencies of dangerous and decided kind. Arrest him on sight.

name: Bird, Andrew Reid
motto: "Dressed in a little brief authority, Most ignorant of what he's most assured." —Hudibras
clubs: Manager Banjo, Mandolin and Glee Clubs, '00-'01
Executive Council Y. M. C. A., '01
description: Very smooth and soft voice and gentle persuasive manner. Disguised as a missionary, frequents Sunday-school picnics and church fairs, where he plies a light-fingered trade. Was arrested last at Hannah More Academy with following articles on his person: four lace handkerchiefs, six silver hat-pins, one pair garters, powder box, eleven silver buckles, and a package of chewing gum.

name: Booker, John Manning
motto: "Ah! why did he write poetry?" —Pope.
clubs: Delegate to J. H. U. A. A., '99-'00
description: Lean, lanky, and generally underfed. Known to the profession as a "dead beat," and will bum anything from a cigarette-paper to a free-lunch. Has some facility at writing and drives lucrative business in forging testimonials for patent medicine almanacs. In his palmy days contributed to Police Gazette, Fireside Companion, and The Metropolitan. Arrested by Anthony Comstock Society, charged with authorship of The Real Sappho. Now out on bail.

name: Norman Boyer
motto: "Fresh as a bridegroom; and his chin new-reaped Show'd like a stubble-land at harvest time." —Shakespeare.
clubs: University Correspondent "Baltimore Morning Herald," '98-'99, '99-'00, '00-'01, and Baltimore Star," '00-'01
description: Slight, angular figure; massive forehead; grave, portentous manner and ministerial voice. Most easily recognized by razor-gashes about jaw and chin. Frequents Pratt Library and was often seen by HULLABALOO agent conversing with the clerks. His whole bearing at such times was so suspicious that the agents feel convinced that his object may or may not have been matrimony, but certainly was not the borrowing of books.

name: John Straith Briscoe
motto: "What rage for fame attends both great and small! Better be damned than mentioned not at all." —John Wolcott.
clubs: President Bryan Inter-Collegiate Association of Maryland, '00-'01
description: Suspected of meaning well. Short man, with aggressive manner and a protesting, careworn look. Presence can be easily detected by the frequent recurrence of the monosyllables "I" and "Why," pitched in a high, worn-out key. Danger from latent criminality of the man greatly enhanced by his ambition which "stops at nothing."

name: Brown, Ernest Bonsall
motto: "I have not loved the world, nor the world me." —Byron.
description: Thin body, chubby face. Just before entering college was put into a hypnotic trance from which he could be but partially awakened. Occasionally shows slight signs of animation, but is chronically apathetic, even at banquets. Drinks by reflex action. In youth seriously injured his frontal cortex causing desperate pessimism. At present, quiet and inoffensive, but will need watching if his mind is restored.

name: Bruton, Alfred William
motto: "'Tis better to be brief than tedious." —Shakespeare.
clubs: Delegate to J. H. U. A. A., '00-'01
Secretary Senior Class Tug-of-war Team, Champions, '99
description: A member of the Sayler Gang. Big, beefy, and brawny. While in the act of making a haul was captured and collar-bone fractured; ball lodged in stomach. Served ten days in House of Correction. Is variously charged with assault and battery.

name: Mortimer Bye
motto: "Vain as the leaf upon the stream, And fickle as a changeful dream, Fantastic as a woman's mood."—Scott.
description: Accused of frightening ladies by waving his handkerchief and otherwise attempting to flirt. Reckless, dare-devil character, afraid of nothing. Very dangerous. Caught in the act of attempting suicide by dressing to within an inch of his life.

name: Harry Stephenson Byrne
motto: "He never has committed himself in his life."—Thackeray. "No wher so bisy a man as he there was, And yet he semed bisier than he was."—Chaucer.
clubs: Assistant Manager Varsity Lacrosse Team, '99-'00, and Manager, '00-'01
Assistant Business Manager "News-Letter," '99-'00, and Business Manager, '00
Delegate to Inter-Collegiate Lacrosse Association, '00, and Secretary and Treasurer, '00
Treasurer National Association of College Journalists, '00-'01
Chairman Committee of Arrangements Inter-Class Debate, '01
description: Some-time reporter on The Daily Damnation. Convicted of malicious slander, plaintiff awarded verdict of thirteen cents. Defendant committed for three months in default. Well known as a ward-heeler, stands in with "The Ring" and has served six terms for intimidating voters.

name: Constantine, K. A. Cosey Walker
motto: "A lion among ladies is a most dreadful thing." —Midsummer-Night's Dream. "And thou art long and lank and brown As is the ribbed sea sand." —Coleridge.
description: A long, lean, lanky lover, frequenting the Woman's College, Baltimore. Exposes, presumably for sale, various female articles, including locks of hair, rings, handkerchiefs, fans, etc., which have attracted the suspicion of the agency. Is not suspected of serious intentions but is a disorganizing element. Hails from the sunny south pursued by dozens of breach-of-promise suits. Conceals his intrigues by a perpetual grin, and works his game evidently by induction.

name: Dapprich, Frederick Richard
motto: "Often the cockloft is empty in those whom Nature hath built many stories high." —Fuller.
description: Desired by Milwaukee authorities at instigation of Schlitz. Demanded by G. C. Lee for unlawful concealment of absences as secretary of the House. Has one ear for music and one for the dinner-bell, and usually carries with him his "fiddle," banjeaurine, and piano; can play "Die Wacht am Rheine" on a drum. The only charge against him is as stated above from the West, probably for two or more litres of dark, unpaid for. Not a member of the "Nation" gang.

name: Duck, Robert Sergeant
motto: "A huge idolator of monosyllables."—Swift.
description: The notorious illustrator of Booker's Side Lights on the Tenderloin, has hair in three shades of red, and is addicted to "hitting the pipe." Was rescued from a wild career in the Latin Quarter, where he went while quite young to study Art and the raison d'etre of Bohemia. Has since picked up a living in drawing posters for Sam T. Jack's "Creoles," which are regularly suppressed by the Anthony Comstock Society. An old hod will furnish sufficient evidence for identification.

name: Ember, Aaron
motto: "Well versed was he in Hebrew books, There was a mystery in his looks, His eyes seemed gazing far away."—Longfellow.
description: Of remarkably studious and industrious appearance. Has several times worked the Come-all-the-way-from-oppressed-Poland-to-complete-my-education-in-this-land-of-liberty game with phenomenal success. By the above method once known to touch Christhilf's heart to the extent of a whole day's earnings of the slot machine. Great astuteness always observable in his operations.

name: Leyburn Grear Fishack
motto:
"Still run on, Poets, in a raging vein,
E'en to the dregs and squeezings of the brain;
Strain out the last dull droppings of their sense,
And rhyme with all the rage of impotence."
—Pope.
"Should every creature drink but I?
Why, man of morals, tell me why?"
—Cowley.
clubs: Holder of 'Varsity and State Record for Pole Vault
description: Fair-haired, scholarly-looking personage, with immature artistic tendencies. Agile-limbed, and has been known as a vaulter (specialty, safe-deposit and cemetery crack work). Served five years in Baltimore City College for incorrigibility. Now out on bail for systematic defrauding of the Barbers' Union.

name: John Frederick Gray
motto:
"In much and deep conversation with himself."
—Swift.
"There is a false Gravity that is a very ill symptom; and it may be said, that as rivers, which run very slowly, have always the most mud at the bottom; so a solid Stiffness in the constant course of a man's life, is a sign of a thick bed of Mud at the bottom of his brain."
—Saville.
description: Is held to appear before Justice Poe charged with suspicious actions in the library. Very little is known of the accused. His conversation is characterized by considerable reticence, especially in the Dean's class. It is expected that the trial will develop some startling information. Rumor has it that he is a busted English earl.

name: Harris, William Hall
motto:
"You beat your pate and fancy wit will come,
Knock as you please, there's nobody at home."—Pope.
"And, when you stick on conversation's burrs,
Don't strew your pathway with those dreadful urs."—Holmes.
"A man who could make so vile a pun would not scruple to pick a pocket."
—Dennis.
description: Short man with kinky, curling locks and an aggressive manner. Bobs his head back when speaking and makes superfluous gestures and remarks with his lips. Easily known by a loud voice, a louder laugh, and the loudest pair of socks. Was observed to cut the last period six Wednesdays in succession, and when followed by a "HULLABALOO" sleuth-hound was tracked to the Howard Auditorium. As our agent wore the badge of the anti-vice society, he was denied entrance to that joint, and thus not being able to follow the clue to the finish, despite strong suspicions, can lay no definite charge against him.

name: Hatch, Ernest Cummins
motto:
"Come, I know you are bashful."—Beaumont and Fletcher.
"Let it be, let it pass."—Gournay.
description: Is addicted to the Lacrosse habit. Beyond this, and his youthful appearance and quiet demeanor nothing much is known. Believed to be a student, but his speech, what there is of it and such as it is, does not betray him.

name: Hollander, Lee Milton
motto: "Disperse these clouds and melancholy looks." —Kyd's "Spanish Tragedy."
description: Strong German accent, but does not drink beer, shows fondness for good poetry, and in other ways tries to conceal German origin. Suspected of being noted anarchist who has come to America with intention of killing Mackdermott and McKinley.

name: Kohn, Walter Isaac
motto: "Seldom he smiles, and smiles in such a sort, As if he mock'd himself, and scorned his spirit, That could be moved to smile at anything." —Shakespeare.
description: Dark-haired, light-fingered, and of a persuasive manner. Great success in fleecing strangers with stories of his breeding farm in Kentucky. Acquitted of running an illegal still in the mountains, but now under indictment for carrying a bowie-knife and loaded gun on the streets of Baltimore.

name: Eugene Joseph Leopold
motto: "That jewel'd mass of millinery." —Tennyson.
description: Wears a great deal of jewelry which has aroused the suspicions of our agents. His presence can be at once detected by a strong scent of Posner's cologne. Affects a slouching gait and a jocular, debonair manner which has a hitch somewhere. Loud-mouthed, and extremely appreciative of the funny things which he thinks he says.

name: John Gresham Machen
motto: "And e'en his failings lean'd to virtue's side." —Goldsmith.
clubs: Chairman and Speaker House Debating Team (winning)
Chairman and Speaker Senate Debating Team (winning)
Executive Council, Y. M. C. A., '00–'01
description: Accused by the overwhelming majority of his class-mates who do not take the scholarships offered, of obtaining under false pretenses, $150.00 annually from the Johns Hopkins University. Hullabaloo Bureau's agents hope to be in command of sufficient proof to convict suspected before June 15th of this year. Personality of accused bears few of the outward marks of the scholarship-taker, besides a preoccupied manner and a pronounced bagginess of the trousers. Accused a leading member of the Y. M. C. A., and a victim of ginger-ale habit.

name: Noble, Edward Richards
motto: "The worst of Madmen is a saint run mad."—Pope.
clubs: Executive Council Y. M. C. A., '99-'00
description: This small young man (physically) has a very smooth and affable exterior; marked powers of fascination, to which silver spoons and similar trivial articles have been known to succumb. Inclined to trifle with the affections of the unsuspecting and is wanted in three states and the Woman's College for breach of promise. Detain and telegraph Mister C. Ritchlow, his guardian.

name: Powell, Milton Williams
motto: "Content thyself and do not trouble me." —Kyd's "Spanish Tragedy."
description: Arrest caused by the Audubon Society of the Woman's College of Baltimore, is held for action of the Grand Jury on the infamous charge of raising chickens without a mother hen. Has lived a shiftless sort of life, and the business public is warned against extending him credit. Is implicated in some very questionable real-estate deals, now under investigation.

name: Raffel, Harry B.
motto: "Sacred to Ridicule his whole life long." —Pope.
description: Wears heavy wig of curly black hair, glasses, gold chain, and so on. Talks with assurance and ceaselessly, with endless illustrations from an imaginary personal experience. Reads scented letters in lavender envelopes during lecture hours. Is suspected of being a woman in disguise investigating Hopkins' methods for the New Woman's Reform Club.

name: Rehberger, George Edward
motto: "Like an old patriarch he appeared, Abraham or Isaac, or at least Some later prophet or high priest." —Longfellow.
description: It is believed that he carries his hands in his pockets for the purpose of manipulating concealed weapons, though he has never been known to draw (upon anyone). Walks with a kangaroo, back-action movement like the kick of a hay-rake. Has that heavy under jaw and close-cropped red hair which so often marks the criminal.

name: Riggins, John Adelbert
motto: "Frank, haughty, rash,—the Rupert of debate." —Lytton.
clubs: Second Vice-President Y. M. C. A., '99-'00
description: Northern carpet-bagger with suave demeanor. Claims to be a Frenchman and speaks Parisian slang fluently. Frequents country stores and attempts to incite the farmers to socialism. Indicted for selling horse boots to farmers without license and obtaining money under pretense of founding a college.

name: Sayler, Jacob Abner
motto: "Framed to make women false." —Othello. "My only books Were women's looks, And folly's all they've taught me." —Moore. "Egregiously an ass." —Othello.
clubs: Delegate to the Alumni Athletic Board, '99-'00
description: The notorious leader of the Sayler Gang of eleven roughs that operate in and about Baltimore. This dare-devil aggregation has been chased from point to point. Its leader is tricky and has always escaped detention. Information leading to his arrest is desired by authorities at St. John's and elsewhere.

name: Shippen, Lloyd Parker
motto: "The world forgetting, and the world forgot."—Pope.
description: Unfathomable character. Appears and disappears silently and mysteriously. Absolutely nothing known about his occupation. Doubts as to whether he has any. Seen absorbed in a low conversation with Mrs. Cruikshank. This is a most important case, and to intelligent agents with hopes for advancement, the slightest suggestion is sufficient.

name: Shreve, Olen Forrest
motto: "The world knows nothing of its greatest men." —Henry Taylor.
description: Characteristic hay-seed. Says "Craps" for "Crops," and lives on the "Eastern Sho'." Wears spectacles on his nose and trousers where a mule kicked him. Has been committed three times for bagging chickens, and is liable to turn up at county fairs.

name: Shriver, William Payne
motto:
"A little learning is a dang'rous thing;
Drink deep, or taste not the Pierian spring:
There shallow draughts intoxicate the brain,
And drinking largely sobers us again."—Pope.
clubs: Member Board of Directors J. H. U. A. A., '98-'99 (resigned)
Executive Council Y. M. C. A., '99-'00, '00-'01
description: He draweth out the thread of his verbosity finer than the staple of his argument.—Shakespeare. Alias, PAIN, alias, GLITTERS.—Rank imposter, and a leading member of the Y. M. C. A. Glittering exterior and brazen confidence which frequently throws the agent off the track. Spent some time in collecting subscriptions for the Y. M. C. A. from innocent Freshmen at the Johns Hopkins University, where he is, whom he had persuaded to join that institution. At present engaged in a determined effort to bluff the faculty into granting him a degree.

name: Snavely, Guy Everett
motto:
"A youth was there of quiet ways,
A student of old books and days."—Longfellow.
description: Stoop-shouldered youth, with the virgin bloom of innocence upon his face. Hails from "up the creek." Devoted himself to fishing as the occupation requiring least labor. Taught infant class in a college in Southern Maryland, and later run out of the county by the vigilance committee for attempting to thrash one of his pupils. Says he enjoys going to Hopkins "more than pitchin' hay."

name: Spencer, Lindsay Coleman
motto: "A gentle dulness ever loves a joke."—Pope.
description: Constantly accused of attempting to crack a joke. Beyond a suspicious method of buttonholing, there is absolutely no foundation for such an accusation. The frock coat and high hat which of late bedeck his tall, angular person, have aroused such suspicion that the agency has begun a thorough investigation of the pawn shops.

name: Stein, Julian Samuel
motto: "There are a sort of men, whose visages Do cream and mantle, like a standing pool."—Shakespeare.
description: Attempted to blow the froth off with a revolver, but succeeded only in mangling his otherwise classic features. Placed in keeping at a thoroughly reliable establishment on Howard and Ross Streets, from which he escaped after a short confinement, during which he wrote an economic thesis in collaboration with Raffel (Cell No. 77) on "Interviews with Factory Hands After Working Hours," and "Reflections on the Effect of City Life Upon Moral Character."

name: Straus, Henry Philip
motto: "Too civil by half."—Sheridan.
description: Fixed and prominent grin; plenty of time swagger. Suspected of body-checking. Habits contracted on the Lacrosse Team make him a tricky man and a fast runner. In spite of lethargic manner and slow movements, he has never been caught napping.

name: Struth, Otto Albert
motto: "When I beheld this I sighed and said within myself: Surely, mortal man is a broomstick."—Swift.
description: Lean and hungry looking. Being caught in the stock-room by Mrs. Stewart with unsigned chemicals upon his person, he hastily swallowed some P₁D₂Q₃ used for cleaning purposes, in order to conceal the theft. He did not conceal it long, however, and upon recovery his hair was found to have turned red. If agent uses a green eye-shade, the felon is easy to see through. Accused only of petty larcenies, and is really quite a harmless, simple creature.

name: Swindell, Walter B.
motto: "A man of ready smile and facile tear, Improvised hopes, despairs at nod and beck, And language—ah, the gift of eloquence!"—Browning. "He had a head to contrive, a tongue to persuade, and a hand to execute any mischief."—Clarendon. "He trudged along, unknowing what he sought, And whistled as he went, for want of thought."—Dryden.
description: Dresses in flashy clothes obtained under false pretenses. Especially skillful at shell game and three-card monte which he operates on trains. Pretends to be a stranded actor. Was last seen in or about the Rathskeller and should be arrested on suspicion.

name: Tyler, James Edward
motto: "He lived in that ideal world Whose language is not speech, but song."—Longfellow. "For my voice, I have lost it with the hollaing and singing of anthems." —Shakespeare. "A Babe in the house is a well-spring of pleasure."—Tupper.
clubs: Assistant Manager 'Varsity Football Team, '99-'00, and Manager, '00-'01 (resigned)
description: Another member of the Sayler gang (see under Sayler). Big frame, muscular, massive jaw. Particularly tough character. Was last held up at Union Park for stealing twenty-five yards. Is wanted for similar offenses at Westminster and Annapolis.

name: Warner, Henry Michael
motto: "So sweetly mawkish, and smoothly dull."—Pope.
description: A soft, meek, patient, humble, tranquil spirit. Well-formed, graceful man, broad-shouldered when he has his overcoat on. Generally wears stilts. Pays much attention to dress, and has written a book on "The Decadence of Propriety." Served four years for forgery of a type-written essay on psychology.

name: West, Augustus Price
motto: "Did you hear that boy laugh?"—Browning.
description: Escaped from the Annheuser Busch Hospital for Consumptives of Beer, and if found can at once be detected by a hectic, rheumatic laugh which sounds like a lawn-mower or a dollar watch being wound up. Treated frequently (at various places) with every known Malt Extract but without stopping the disease. Should be detained on sight and returned to the Hospital with as little fuss as possible. N. B.—Very genteel and of an ingratiating manner.

name: Whitman, Horace Slingluff
motto: "An abridgment of all that was pleasant in man."—Goldsmith.
clubs: Delegate to J. H. U. A. A., '00-'01
description: Accused of obtaining silverware from sprinters by stealing on the pistol. Centre of the Sayler gang of eleven roughs who assaulted and badly beat certain Maryland University students. A self-contained and hardened criminal, who has no respect for the rights of Dean or man.

name: Wolf Jr., Marcus Wilton
motto: "Thou sayest an undisputed thing in such a solemn way."—Holmes.
description: From a delicate hint thrown out by him at a banquet, he is suspected of "feeling himself a prominent member of the Class of Nineteen-One"—Rogues Gallery. Accused must be innocent, as there is no foundation for any such feeling on his part. Agency believes this is a thoroughly harmless character.
